body.title: NOOP_COMMENT opcode — inline comments in habit payloads
body.result: NOOP_COMMENT opcode added to node_executor known-set; runtime no-op, text logged at DEBUG. 5 new tests, 4149 regression pass.
body.description:
Habit payloads currently have no in-line commenting mechanism. Add a NOOP_COMMENT opcode to node_executor that the runtime skips during execution but preserves in the payload for human readability. Lets habit authors leave breadcrumbs about WHY a particular branch/check exists without misusing other opcodes.

Shape: opcode='NOOP_COMMENT', args={'text': '...'}. Runtime treats as no-op (continues to next opcode). Linter/printer surfaces them to humans.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/node_executor.py (add opcode handler), tests/test_node_executor.py (add coverage).
**Design rules:** none apply.
**Scope boundary:** in scope: opcode + handler + tests. Out of scope: retroactive comment injection on existing habits, UI for surfacing comments.
**Test plan:** unit tests: NOOP_COMMENT runs without affecting basket state; chain with NOOP_COMMENT in middle preserves all other opcode results.
